# Agentic Code Review: ovid/first-pass

**Date:** 2026-06-06 19:54:22
**Branch:** `ovid/first-pass` -> `main`
**Commit:** `da4fb97d9b562ad58128bca5a98312fccad6cdfd`
**Files changed:** 77 total (46 hand-written files reviewed; vendored/copied/generated files excluded per instruction)
**Lines changed:** +104,988 / -1 total (~2,466 hand-written lines in scope)
**Diff size category:** Large

> **Scope note:** Per the review instruction, files copied from `.gitignore`d directories
> were **not** reviewed: `vendor/glkote/glkapi.js`, `public/games/*.z3` (story files),
> `tests/fixtures/glkote-zork1-boot.json` (87k-line captured protocol fixture), and the
> Vite boilerplate SVGs (`src/assets/*.svg`). These were read **only** as references to
> verify contracts, never reported against. Review targets the new TypeScript/TSX source,
> the protocol-capture script, and the build/test configuration.

## Executive Summary

This first-pass implementation is **architecturally sound and well-aligned with the plan**:
the GlkOte injection seam, native-autosave persistence model, locked theme tokens, and the
self-hosted-font/no-CDN privacy promise are all honored, and the security review found **no
actionable issues**. The highest-priority problem is a **two-part crash on the common
story-fetch path** ([C1]): a missing or mis-served `.z3` file produces unhandled rejections
and feeds a non-story byte buffer into the VM, because there is no `r.ok`/length validation
anywhere in the `fetch → signature → boot` pipeline. The other confirmed bugs are an
untested transcript-corruption-on-`clear` reducer gap, an intermittent autosave write-ordering
race ("resume a turn behind"), and several quality/latent issues. Overall confidence in the
codebase is good; the Critical issue should be fixed before this is exercised against any
environment where a game file could be missing.

## Critical Issues

### [C1] Missing/404'd story file crashes boot — no fetch validation, no length guard

- **File:** `src/ui/App.tsx:21-22` and `:30-31` (fetch); `src/zmachine/signature.ts:6` (unguarded index)
- **Bug:** `fetch(game.file)` is passed straight to `.arrayBuffer()` with **no `r.ok` check and no `.catch`**. `fetch` does not reject on HTTP 404 — it resolves with `ok:false` and an HTML error body, which flows into `signature()` and the VM. Separately, `signature.ts:6` does `bytes[i].toString(16)` for `i < 0x1e` with no bounds check, so any buffer shorter than 30 bytes (truncated download, error page) throws `TypeError: Cannot read properties of undefined`. The resume-discovery `Promise.all` has no `.catch` (unhandled rejection on mount if any game 404s), and `enter()` is an async onClick whose rejection is also unhandled.
- **Impact:** On any environment where a `.z3` file is missing or mis-served, the landing screen's resume hints silently fail to populate **and** clicking "Light the lamp" either crashes with an unhandled rejection or boots the VM on garbage bytes. Common-path failure with no user-visible error.
- **Suggested fix:** In both fetch sites, check `if (!r.ok) throw new Error(...)`, wrap in try/catch, and surface a load error in the UI. In `signature.ts`, guard `if (bytes.length < 0x1e) throw new Error('story too short')`.
- **Confidence:** High (85–88)
- **Found by:** Error Handling (F6, F5), Security (noted F5), Contract (noted F5)

## Important Issues

### [I1] Reducer ignores `clear:true` — RESTART / screen clears corrupt the transcript

- **File:** `src/glkote-react/reduce.ts:126-137` (buffer-content branch)
- **Bug:** The buffer reducer reads only `entry.text` and never inspects `entry.clear`. Verified against `vendor/glkote/glkapi.js:600-608`: `glk_window_clear` sets `obj.clear = true` on the buffer content object. The reducer always appends onto cumulative `lineTexts`, so a clear is silently dropped — the entire prior transcript stays and the fresh first paragraph (which arrives `append:true`) merges onto a now-stale trailing line.
- **Impact:** In-game `RESTART` (and menu/clear screens) corrupt the visible transcript. The design spec wants RESTART to work after game-end. This path has no fixture or unit test.
- **Suggested fix:** In the buffer branch, when `entry.clear` is truthy, reset `lineTexts = []` (treating subsequent lines as new) before processing `entry.text`.
- **Confidence:** High (88)
- **Found by:** Logic, Contract

### [I2] Echoed-command input style is discarded — `kind:'input'` render branch is dead

- **File:** `src/glkote-react/reduce.ts:91-94` (`classify`) + `:12-19` (`joinRunText`); dead branch at `src/ui/Scrollback.tsx:24,42-49`
- **Bug:** `classify()` only ever returns `'room'`/`'output'`, so nothing produces `kind:'input'`. Per `PROTOCOL-NOTES.md`, echoed player commands arrive as a run with style `"input"`, but `joinRunText` keeps only odd-indexed text and discards the style name. The `l.kind === 'input'` caret/`.echo` branch in `Scrollback` is therefore permanently unreachable, and echoed commands render as plain output with no `>` caret.
- **Impact:** The intended input-echo styling never appears (reachable every turn). Not a crash.
- **Suggested fix:** Thread each run's style through `bufferParagraphs`/`joinRunText` and classify a paragraph whose first run style is `"input"` as `kind:'input'`.
- **Confidence:** High (80)
- **Found by:** Logic, Contract (two specialists — same bug)

### [I3] Autosave write-ordering race — can persist a stale snapshot ("resume a turn behind")

- **File:** `src/storage/idb.ts:13-30,33` + `src/storage/dialog.ts:83-104`
- **Bug:** `autosave_write` is fire-and-forget (`op.catch(...)` only logs; nothing awaits), and each `idbSet` calls `tx()` → `open()`, opening its **own** connection + transaction, then `db.close()`. IndexedDB only guarantees commit ordering for transactions created on the **same** connection; two same-key writes from consecutive turns run on independent connections with no ordering guarantee. A later turn's snapshot can be overtaken by an earlier in-flight write, persisting a stale snapshot. The in-memory sync `cache` always holds the latest, so the divergence only surfaces across a page reload.
- **Impact:** Intermittent "resume a turn behind" / save corruption after reload — exactly the class of symptom behind the recent "Fix autosave" commits. Hidden in-session by the cache.
- **Suggested fix:** Serialize writes per key through a single promise chain (`this.writeChain = this.writeChain.then(() => idbSet/idbDel(...))`), or reuse one long-lived connection with a serial queue.
- **Confidence:** Medium (72)
- **Found by:** Concurrency (prime suspect)

### [I4] Test files are never type-checked

- **File:** `tsconfig.json` (`files:[]` + references) → `tsconfig.app.json:25-30` (excludes `*.test.ts(x)`), `tsconfig.node.json` (includes only `vite.config.ts`)
- **Bug:** `make typecheck` runs `tsc --noEmit`/`tsc -b` against the solution `tsconfig.json`, whose referenced projects exclude all test files and never include `vitest.config.ts`. No test file (including the new `shared-buffer.test.ts`) is ever type-checked.
- **Impact:** Type errors in the test suite pass CI silently — a real gap given the project's "TDD throughout" convention.
- **Suggested fix:** Add a test-scoped tsconfig project (with vitest globals types) covering `src/**/*.test.*` + `vitest.config.ts`, referenced from the root `tsconfig.json`.
- **Confidence:** High (80)
- **Found by:** Contract

## Suggestions

- **[S1]** `src/zmachine/shared-buffer.test.ts:55` — stale `// currently MISSES` comment on an assertion that now **passes** after the `engine.ts:93` `storyBytes.slice()` fix; misleading / false-green risk. Remove it. _(Logic, conf 80)_
- **[S2]** `src/glkote-react/reduce.ts:92` — `classify()` heuristic `/^[A-Z][^.!?]{2,40}$/` misclassifies ordinary capitalized lines ("Opened", lines with commas/colons/quotes) as room headings. Cosmetic (CSS class only). _(Error Handling, conf 72)_
- **[S3]** `src/glkote-react/bridge.ts:93` — `if (this.view.ended) this.onEnd?.()` fires on _every_ update once `ended` latches true (reducer never resets it); end is keyed solely on `exit===true` with no `disable`+empty-input fallback. Guard `onEnd` to fire once. _(Error Handling, conf 68)_
- **[S4]** `src/ui/App.tsx:17-26` — resume-hint `savedSlugs` is computed once on a `[]`-dep effect; returning to Landing via `onChangeVolume` (App stays mounted) never refreshes the hint (misses a new save / shows a cleared slot). Also no cancellation guard, so `setSavedSlugs` can fire after unmount. _(Contract, Concurrency — two specialists, conf 65)_
- **[S5]** `src/storage/idb.ts:13-30` (`tx`) + `:4-11` (`open`) — handlers are attached to the request only; no `transaction.onabort` and no `open().onblocked`. On a quota-exceeded abort or version-blocked open the wrapping promise can hang. Add `t.onabort = () => reject(t.error)` and `req.onblocked`. _(Error Handling, conf 65)_
- **[S6]** `src/glkote-react/reduce.ts:3` — `let nextId = 1` is a module-global shared across all reducer/engine instances; under StrictMode/two concurrent engines the React line `key`s become coupled and non-deterministic. Carry the counter per-reducer-stream. _(Concurrency, conf 65)_
- **[S7]** `src/ui/Terminal.tsx:22-38` — the boot effect cleanup only sets `cancelled`/nulls `engineRef`; it never tears down the engine (no `dispose()`), so a StrictMode throwaway engine stays live with autosave enabled and can write the same IDB key (compounds with [I3]). The `engine.ts:93` `.slice()` fix prevents only the signature-divergence aspect. Add an explicit `dispose()` and call it from cleanup. _(Concurrency, conf 65)_

## Plan Alignment

Plan/design docs were found and consulted. Overall alignment: **High.** All five milestones are implemented; the Milestone 1 walking-skeleton gate was cleared via the custom GlkOte bridge (the stock-`glkote.js` fallback was not needed). TDD discipline is visible (co-committed tests; ~46 commits roughly one-per-step). `glkapi.js` is vendored and SHA-pinned; `ifvms` is consumed from npm, never the gitignored checkout. Theme tokens are copied verbatim from the locked spec, fonts are self-hosted (no CDN refs), and the theme toggle is in-layout (never `position:fixed`).

- **Implemented:** Scaffolding (M0); walking-skeleton + protocol capture + reducer + bridge + engine (M1); IndexedDB Dialog + native autosave + signature keying (M2); Folio UI + self-hosted fonts + theme tokens (M3); catalog/landing/routing for Zork I/II/III (M4); game-end input handling + named SAVE/RESTORE fileref + README (M5).
- **Not yet implemented:** Nothing material from the first-pass plan. The WebLLM natural-language layer remains correctly out of scope (deferred to `docs/notes.md`). A stray untracked `screeshot.png` sits in the working tree.
- **Deviations:** No hard contradictions. One **stale-spec** note: the design doc (`...naitfol-design.md:101-103,119-120,158-159`) says the bridge/engine calls `engine.do_autosave()` at the turn boundary, but the implementation deliberately relies on **native** `do_vm_autosave` and keeps `onTurn` as an observer-only seam (`bridge.ts:51-63` warns that calling `do_autosave` from `onTurn` would double-save). The code is the better design; **the spec should be corrected** so a future contributor doesn't "restore" the documented behavior and reintroduce double-saving. The MORE auto-ack reduced to a no-op and the GiDispa version-skew shim are both documented, plan-authorized adjustments, not deviations.

## Review Metadata

- **Agents dispatched:** Logic & Correctness, Error Handling & Edge Cases, Contract & Integration, Concurrency & State, Security, Plan Alignment (6 specialists, parallel) + 1 Verifier
- **Scope:** 46 hand-written files (src TS/TSX, `scripts/capture-protocol.mjs`, build/test config) + adjacent test files; vendored `glkapi.js`, fixtures, and `.z3` files read as references only
- **Raw findings:** 17 (plus security clean, plan alignment high)
- **Verified findings:** 13 (1 Critical, 4 Important, 7 Suggestions, 1 spec-correction note)
- **Filtered out:** 4 false positives — F3 (`bytes.buffer` pooling: files >4 KiB get own ArrayBuffer, offset 0), F8 (`open()` _does_ have `onerror`; rejection is surfaced), F9 (no second `preload` call site; absent-value notions are actually consistent), F12 (`flushAutosave` is a documented test convenience; real error surfacing lives in `dialog.autosave_write`'s `op.catch`)
- **Steering files consulted:** `CLAUDE.md` (confirmed self-hosted fonts / no CDN / in-layout theme toggle conventions are honored)
- **Plan/design docs consulted:** `docs/superpowers/plans/2026-06-06-naitfol-first-pass.md`, `docs/superpowers/specs/2026-06-06-naitfol-design.md`, `docs/spikes/2026-06-06-glk-vite-spike.md`, `tests/fixtures/PROTOCOL-NOTES.md`
